description:
You are a backup administrator preparing the system for the next monthly archive cycle.
    Two existing configuration files need to be updated and a log of your changes must be kept.  
    
    1. Files and locations  
       • YAML file:  /home/user/backups/conf/backup.yaml  
       • TOML file:  /home/user/backups/conf/storage.toml  
       • Change-log: /home/user/backups/backup_edit.log (append-only text file)

    2. Required edits  
       A. /home/user/backups/conf/backup.yaml  
          • Change the value of the top-level key “compression” from **lz4** to **zstd**.  
          • Change the value of “retention_days” from **30** to **45**.  
       B. /home/user/backups/conf/storage.toml  
          • Under the [storage] table change “bucket” from **company-backups** to **archive-backups**.  
          • Under the [credentials] table set  
               access_key = "ARCHIVE_KEY"  
               secret_key = "ARCHIVE_SECRET"  

    3. Logging requirement  
       Immediately after applying the edits, append exactly four lines to  
       /home/user/backups/backup_edit.log — one line per change listed above.  
       Each line must follow this precise format so that automated tests can match it:  

           YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - <description text>  

       • The date-time stamp must be the current local system time when the edit is made.  
       • The description text must be exactly the following four sentences (one per line):  
           compression field updated from lz4 to zstd  
           retention_days updated from 30 to 45  
           bucket updated from company-backups to archive-backups  
           credentials updated  

       Example of a correctly formatted single line (your timestamp will differ):  
           2025-04-02 14:07:13 - compression field updated from lz4 to zstd  

    4. Verification expectations  
       After you finish, the automated checker will confirm:  
       • /home/user/backups/conf/backup.yaml contains the keys schedule, directories, compression, and retention_days, with compression: "zstd" and retention_days: 45 exactly as shown below.  
       • /home/user/backups/conf/storage.toml contains the two tables [storage] and [credentials] with the new bucket and credentials.  
       • /home/user/backups/backup_edit.log exists and its last 4 lines match the required pattern and text.  

    5. Notes & hints  
       • Keep the existing YAML indentation (2 spaces).  
       • Preserve the order of the keys already present in each file; only modify the requested values.  
       • If any of the directories in the paths above don’t exist yet, create them with standard user permissions.  
       • You should only need a small set of shell utilities (e.g., mkdir, sed, echo, date, etc.) to complete everything.  
       • Do not delete or rename any of the existing files.
truth:
<!--
      Initial files present before the agent starts.
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <file path="/home/user/backups/conf/backup.yaml"><![CDATA[
schedule: "03:00"
directories:
  - /var/www
  - /home/projects
compression: "lz4"
retention_days: 30
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/backups/conf/storage.toml"><![CDATA[
[storage]
type = "s3"
bucket = "company-backups"
region = "us-east-1"

[credentials]
access_key = "REPLACE_ME"
secret_key = "REPLACE_ME"
        ]]></file>

        <!-- backup_edit.log may or may not exist; if it does, it can have any content. -->
    </initial_state>

    <final_state>
        <file path="/home/user/backups/conf/backup.yaml"><![CDATA[
schedule: "03:00"
directories:
  - /var/www
  - /home/projects
compression: "zstd"
retention_days: 45
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/backups/conf/storage.toml"><![CDATA[
[storage]
type = "s3"
bucket = "archive-backups"
region = "us-east-1"

[credentials]
access_key = "ARCHIVE_KEY"
secret_key = "ARCHIVE_SECRET"
        ]]></file>

        <log path="/home/user/backups/backup_edit.log">
            The last 4 lines (ignoring earlier content, if any) must each be 19 timestamp characters,
            the string " - ", and one of the four required description sentences in this exact order:
            1. compression field updated from lz4 to zstd
            2. retention_days updated from 30 to 45
            3. bucket updated from company-backups to archive-backups
            4. credentials updated
        </log>
    </final_state>
